description_long: This is a military jet aircraft with a sleek, elongated fuselage and a pointed nose cone, painted primarily in light gray with U.S. Air Force insignia and markings. The wings are short, straight, and mounted low on the fuselage, with small stabilizers near the tips. The tail section features a prominent T-tail configuration, with a large horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, likely designed for a single pilot. The aircraft is equipped with a single jet engine mounted at the rear, and the landing gear appears to be retractable, with the main gear housed under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit. The overall design suggests a high-speed, high-altitude interceptor or reconnaissance role.
description: A sleek, gray U.S. Air Force jet with a pointed nose, low straight wings, and a prominent T-tail, featuring a single rear jet engine and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, T-tail, and U.S. Air Force markings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed nose, narrow fuselage, and the low, straight wings extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the T-tail and rear engine.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the full wingspan, cockpit placement, and the overall streamlined shape of the aircraft.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with small tip stabilizers and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a high-mounted horizontal stabilizer
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy
